findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
Over-broad keyword baiting in skill description
findings.description: The skill description contains an extensive list of trigger keywords ('plugin settings', 'store plugin configuration', 'user-configurable plugin', '.local.md files', 'plugin state files', 'read YAML frontmatter', 'per-project plugin settings') which could lead to unwanted activation. This is a low-severity discovery/activation concern.
findings.evidence: Description field: 'This skill should be used when the user asks about "plugin settings", "store plugin configuration", "user-configurable plugin", ".local.md files", "plugin state files", "read YAML frontmatter", "per-project plugin settings", or wants to make plugin behavior configurable.'
findings.remediation: Reduce the number of trigger keywords in the description to only the most essential ones to minimize unwanted activation.

findings.title: Missing allowed-tools in manifest
findings.description: The SKILL.md manifest does not declare an 'allowed-tools' field. While this is optional per the spec, its absence means there are no declared tool restrictions to verify against the skill's actual behavior.
findings.evidence: YAML frontmatter contains: name, description, version but no allowed-tools field.
findings.remediation: Consider adding an 'allowed-tools' field to the manifest to explicitly declare which agent tools the skill requires.

findings.title: Missing compatibility field in manifest
findings.description: The SKILL.md manifest does not declare a 'compatibility' field. While optional, this field helps users understand which environments the skill is designed for.
findings.evidence: YAML frontmatter contains: name, description, version but no compatibility field.
findings.remediation: Consider adding a 'compatibility' field to specify supported environments (e.g., Claude.ai, Claude Code, API).
